english: between truth and fable is but vague and unsettled); sgruṅ bśád-pa (sgrung bshad pa) to relate fables, stories etc.; sṅon-rábs sgruṅ (sngon rabs sgrung) Zam., sṅón-gyi sgruṅ-rgyúd (sngon gyi sgrung rgyud) Glr., sgruṅ-gtám (sgrung gtam) tales of ancient times, of the days of yore; sgruṅ-mkán (sgrung mkhan) Cs., sgruṅ-pa (sgrung pa) Sch. the inventor or writer of fables and tales, also a narrator of tales.
pf. bsgruṅs (bsgrungs), fut. bsgruṅ (bsgrung), 1. to mix. 2. to invent, to feign Cs.
pf. and fut. bsgrun (bsgrun) 1. to compare c. la (la) and daṅ (dang) Dzl. — 2. to emulate, vie, contend with Cs.

I. vb. pf. bsgrubs (bsgrubs), fut. bsgrub (bsgrub), imp. sgrub(s) (sgrub(s)) (cf. grúb-pa (grub pa) Ssk. साघ्) 1. to complete, finish, perform, carry out, an order, a wish, hence usually with bźín-du (bzhin du) Dzl.; to make, achieve, manufacture, obtain, attain, dṅúl-rdo-la dṅúl bsgrúb-tu btúb-pa ltar (dngul rdo la dngul bsgrub tu btub pa ltar) séms-ċan-la (sems can la) Saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas) bsgrúl-tu (bsgrul tu) btúb-pa yín-no (btub pa yin no) in like manner as silver is obtained from silver-ore, Buddha may proceed from beings Thgy.; don sgrúb-pa (don sgrub pa) to attain to one's aim, to obtain a blessing, a boon; tse ˳dii don sgrúb-pa (tshe 'di'i don sgrub pa) to care for the wants of this life; to procure, rgyágs-pýe (rgyags phye) flour, as provision for a journey Mil.; nor sgrúb-pa (nor sgrub pa) to gain riches; to furnish with, to supply, one's self or others Mil. — 2. lha-sgrúb-pa (lha sgrub pa) implies, in accordance to Brahmanic-Buddhistic theology, not so much the making a deity propitious to man (Cs.), as rendering a god subject to human power, forcing him to perform the will of man. This coercion of a god seems to be effected in a twofold manner. The practise of the common people is to perform a vast amount of prayers and conjurations, specially intended for the god that is to be made subject. Another method is adopted by saints, who are advanced in every kind of religious knowledge. They continue their sgóm-pa (sgom pa), or profound meditation, for months and years, until the deity, finally overcome, stands before them visible and tangible, nay, until they have been personally united with and, as it were, incorporated into the invoked and subjected god. Whilst the conatus, the labouring in his arduous undertaking, is often called sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa), the arriving at the proposed end is designated by grúb-pa (grub pa), e.g. rgyál-pos rta-mgrín sgrúb-pa mdzád-pas grúb-nas rta-skád btón-pas (rgyal pos rta mgrin sgrub pa mdzad pas grub nas rta skad bton pas) etc., the king began to coerce Taḍín (Hayagrīwa), and when the latter was made obsequious, so as to appear, a neighing was heard etc. Glr.; sgom-sgrúb byéd-pa (sgom sgrub byed pa) for sgóm-pa daṅ sgrúb-pa byéd-pa (sgom pa dang sgrub pa byed pa) Mil. — bsgrub-k'aṅ (bsgrub khang), sgrub-gnás (sgrub gnas), sgrub-p'úg (sgrub phug) the house, the place, the cavern, where a saint applies himself to sgrúb-pa (sgrub pa); sgrúb-pa-po (sgrub pa po) one effectuating the coercion described above, Sambh. frq. — sgrub-rtágs (sgrub rtags) tokens, proofs of perfection, of an accomplished saint. — sgrub-t'ábs (sgrub thabs) the method of effecting the coercion, of obliging a god to make his appearance; sgrub-byéd (sgrub byed) 1. he that accomplishes the coercion (cf. Schl. p. 247). 2. a kind of bile Med. — sgrub-gśén (sgrub gshen) the Bon-doctrine Mil. II. sbst. 1. one that contemplates and meditates, like sgom-čén (sgom chen) Mil. 2. n of a sect of Lamas, with whom marriage is permitted.

I. Cs. adj. naked, gen. sgren-mo (sgren mo). II. vb. pf. bsgres (bsgres), fut. bsgre (bsgre) (cf. ˳gre-ba ('gre ba)) 1. to roll Glr., Pth. — 2. to multiply Wdk. — 3. to repeat Cs. — 4. to put or place in order, to put together, to compare, e.g. records Tar. 174, 14 Schf.
1. vb. pf. sgregs (sgregs), to belch. — 2. sbst. belch, eructation, sgrég-pa ˳dón-pa (sgreg pa 'don pa), ˳byín-pa ('byin pa) Med. *rul-dág* (rul dag) C. a belch of a fetid smell.

pf. bsgreṅs (bsgrengs), fut. bsgreṅ (bsgreng), imp. sgreṅ(s) (sgreng(s)), cf. gréṅ-ba (greng ba), 1. to raise, erect, lift up, hold up, plant up, e.g. a finger, a beam etc. — 2. to stretch out a limb and hold it stiff C.
naked, sgrén-mor ˳byúṅ-ba (sgren mor 'byung ba) to appear in a naked state, to show one's self naked Dzl.; Bhar. 59. Schf. 'orbus', orphaned (cog. to bkren? (bkren?)).
